title: 2snipe Integration Manifest
description: Manifest file for a *2snipe integration, enabling discovery and installation by snipemgr.

$schema.description: URL to this JSON Schema — include for editor validation and autocomplete.

name.description: Must match the GitHub repository name exactly (e.g. '1password2snipe').

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in snipemgr list (e.g. '1Password').

description.description: One-line description shown in snipemgr list.

version.description: SemVer string (e.g. '1.2.3') matching the latest GitHub Release tag.

min_snipemgr.description: Minimum snipemgr version required to install this integration.

tags.description: Used for filtering in snipemgr list --filter <tag>.

category.description: Snipe-IT license category this integration's licenses belong to.

config_schema.description: Drives the install wizard. Must be non-empty.

key.description: Dot-notation config key matching the settings.yaml structure (e.g. 'onepassword.api_token').

label.description: Human-readable prompt label shown in the install wizard.

secret.description: If true, the value is masked in the wizard and stored in GCP Secret Manager.

required.description: If true, the wizard rejects empty input.

default.description: Pre-filled value shown in the wizard input.

hint.description: Help text shown below the input field in the wizard.

env_var.description: Environment variable name injected into the Cloud Run Job container. If omitted, snipemgr derives the name from key (e.g. 'github.token' → 'GITHUB_TOKEN'). Set explicitly when the integration's viper binding uses a different name.

shared_config.description: Config key prefixes shared across integrations (e.g. ['snipe_it']). The wizard offers to reuse existing secrets for these prefixes.

commands.description: Declares which standard commands the integration binary supports.

releases.description: Describes how to locate the integration binary in GitHub Releases.

github_releases.description: Must be true — only GitHub Releases are supported.

asset_pattern.description: Asset filename template containing {os} and {arch} tokens (e.g. 'example2snipe_{os}_{arch}'). The .exe extension is appended automatically on Windows.
